mission.title: Install and Configure K3s (Lightweight Kubernetes)
mission.description: K3s is a lightweight Kubernetes distribution designed for resource-constrained environments, making it ideal for edge computing, IoT, and development. It simplifies the installation process by bundling all necessary components into a single binary, but may lack some advanced features found in full Kubernetes distributions.

steps.title: Install K3s Binary
steps.description:
This step installs the K3s binary on your system, which is the core component of K3s.

```bash

curl -sfL https://get.k3s.io | sh -

```

> This command downloads and installs the latest K3s version.

steps.title: Verify K3s Installation
steps.description:
Check that K3s is running correctly by verifying the status of the K3s service.

```bash

systemctl status k3s

```

> This command shows the status of the K3s service.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure kubectl to use the K3s context for managing the cluster.

```bash

export KUBECONFIG=/etc/rancher/k3s/k3s.yaml

kubectl get nodes

```

> This sets the KUBECONFIG environment variable to point to the K3s configuration.
> Verify that kubectl can communicate with the K3s cluster.

steps.title: Deploy a Sample Application
steps.description:
Deploy a simple Nginx application to verify that K3s is functioning as expected.

```bash

kubectl create deployment nginx --image=nginx

kubectl expose deployment nginx --port=80 --type=NodePort

```

> This command creates a deployment running Nginx.
> Expose the Nginx deployment to access it externally.

steps.title: Verify Application Deployment
steps.description:
Check that the Nginx application is running and accessible.

```bash

kubectl get pods

kubectl get svc

```

> This command lists all pods to verify that the Nginx pod is running.
> This command shows the service details, including the NodePort.

uninstall.title: Uninstall K3s
uninstall.description:
This step removes K3s from your system. Be aware that this will delete all resources in the cluster, leading to potential data loss.

```bash

/usr/local/bin/k3s-uninstall.sh

```

> Run this script to uninstall K3s.

upgrade.title: Upgrade K3s
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your cluster data. This step involves draining nodes, upgrading K3s, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

curl -sfL https://get.k3s.io | sh -

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

kubectl get nodes

```

> Drain the node to prepare for upgrade.
> This command upgrades K3s to the latest version.
> Uncordon the node to allow scheduling again.
> Verify that all nodes are in a Ready state after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'k3s is not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The K3s service may not have started correctly.

**Fix:**
Check the logs with 'journalctl -u k3s' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** kubectl may not be installed or not in the PATH.

**Fix:**
Install kubectl using 'curl -LO https://storage.googleapis.com/kubernetes-release/release/$(curl -s https://storage.googleapis.com/kubernetes-release/release/stable.txt)/bin/linux/amd64/kubectl && chmod +x ./kubectl && sudo mv ./kubectl /usr/local/bin/kubectl'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Kubeconfig may not be set correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure KUBECONFIG is set correctly with 'export KUBECONFIG=/etc/rancher/k3s/k3s.yaml'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have any resources deployed.

**Fix:**
Deploy a sample application using 'kubectl create deployment nginx --image=nginx'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for additional storage backends including Postgres, improved metrics server integration.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the use of certain legacy flags in the CLI.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.30, ensure to review the new storage backend configurations.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get nodes' showing your nodes in a Ready state and 'kubectl get pods' showing your applications running.

prerequisites.description: Ensure your system meets the minimum resource requirements and has the necessary tools installed.
